feed.title: Google Cloud: Firestore
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Firestore

entry.content: The Firestore emulator now supports Enterprise edition. See Start emulator in specific edition.
entry.summary: The Firestore emulator now supports the Enterprise edition, allowing developers to test their applications with enterprise-level features. You can find more details on how to start the emulator in a specific edition in the documentation.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore emulator supports Enterprise edition

entry.content: Firestore Enterprise edition in Native mode and the Pipeline operations interface are now supported at the General Availability (GA) level.
entry.summary: Firestore Enterprise edition in Native mode and the Pipeline operations interface have reached General Availability. This signifies their readiness for production workloads and widespread adoption.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore Enterprise GA

entry.content:
Firestore Enterprise edition now supports Text search and Geospatial search.

These features are in Preview.
entry.summary: Firestore Enterprise edition now offers text and geospatial search capabilities, currently in preview. These powerful new features allow for more flexible and advanced data querying within your applications.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore Enterprise: New Search Capabilities in Preview

entry.content: The Firestore remote MCP server is now supported at the General Availability (GA) level.
entry.summary: The Firestore remote MCP server has reached General Availability (GA). This means it is now a stable and production-ready feature for all users.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore MCP server now generally available

entry.content: You can now use pipeline operations to perform joins with subqueries. To learn more, see Perform joins with subqueries.
entry.summary: You can now leverage pipeline operations to execute joins with subqueries. This enhancement allows for more complex data manipulation within your pipelines. Explore the documentation to learn how to implement this new feature.
entry.title: Feature: Pipeline Joins with Subqueries Now Available

entry.content:
Firestore Enterprise edition now supports the update(...) and delete() pipeline operation stages. Use these stages to Modify data with Pipeline operations.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Firestore Enterprise edition now allows you to use `update(...)` and `delete()` within pipeline operations. This feature, currently in Preview, enables more flexible data modification directly within your data pipelines.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore Enterprise supports pipeline updates and deletes

entry.content:
You can now use the Usage Insights dashboard in the Google Cloud console to monitor and analyze your billable usage for specific Firestore databases. Usage insights help you track granular usage data, optimize costs, and monitor historical trends.

To learn more, see the guide to analyze usage insights for Native mode and MongoDB compatibility mode.
entry.summary: The Usage Insights dashboard is now available in the Cloud console, allowing you to monitor and analyze billable usage for your Firestore databases. This feature helps in tracking granular usage data, optimizing costs, and understanding historical trends.
entry.title: Feature: Monitor Firestore Usage

entry.content:
Regional and Multi-Regional endpoints for the Firestore API are now Generally Available (GA). You can use a Regional or a Multi-Regional endpoint to ensure that your application's requests are transmitted, stored and processed in the same region or multi-region as your database's location.

To learn more, see the Firestore regional endpoints guide.

You can also use Private Service Connect regional endpoints and Private Service Connect backends to connect to the regional and the multi-regional endpoints of the Firestore API.
entry.summary: Regional and Multi-Regional endpoints for the Firestore API are now Generally Available. This allows you to ensure your application's requests are transmitted, stored, and processed in the same region or multi-region as your database's location. You can also use Private Service Connect to connect to these endpoints.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore regional and multi-regional endpoints are now GA

entry.content: Firestore Enterprise edition now supports Native mode in all supported regions. For a list of supported regions, see Locations.
entry.summary: Firestore Enterprise edition now supports Native mode across all supported regions. This expansion offers greater flexibility and broader availability for users leveraging this powerful database solution. For a comprehensive list of supported regions, please refer to the official Firestore locations documentation.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore Enterprise Native Mode Expansion

entry.content: After March 17, 2026, when you enable Firestore, the Firestore MCP server is automatically enabled.
entry.summary: Starting March 17, 2026, the Firestore MCP server will be automatically enabled for all new Firestore instances. This change ensures enhanced performance and functionality for your applications from the moment you enable Firestore.
entry.title: Change: Firestore MCP server enabled by default

entry.content: Control of MCP use with organization policies is deprecated. After March 17, 2026, organization policies that use the gcp.managed.allowedMCPServices constraint won't work, and you can control MCP use with IAM deny policies. For more information about controlling MCP use, see Control MCP use with IAM.
entry.summary: Control of MCP use with organization policies is deprecated and will stop working after March 17, 2026. You should transition to using IAM deny policies to manage MCP usage. For detailed instructions, refer to the documentation on controlling MCP use with IAM.
entry.title: Deprecated: MCP Use Control Deprecation

entry.content: New best practices are available for securing generative AI agents using Model Context Protocol (MCP) with Google Cloud databases. This guide covers key security measures like least privilege, native database controls, and secure agent design to help you build safer AI applications. For more information, see Best practices for securing agent interactions with Model Context Protocol.
entry.summary: New best practices guide you on securing generative AI agents using Model Context Protocol (MCP) with Google Cloud databases. Learn about least privilege, native database controls, and secure agent design to build safer AI applications.
entry.title: Announcement: Secure Generative AI Agents with MCP

entry.content:
You can now use the Firestore remote MCP server. The Firestore remote MCP server lets you interact with documents stored in a Firestore database from your AI application.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now use the Firestore remote MCP server to interact with documents stored in a Firestore database from your AI application. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Interact with Firestore from AI apps

entry.content:
The Firestore databases page in the Google Cloud console now includes a status column. Possible statuses include:

  • Ready
  • Cloning is in progress
  • Restoring from backup is in progress
  • Deleted
  • Failed

For the cloning and restore statuses, the status column updates upon completion.
entry.summary: The Firestore databases page now displays a status column, showing statuses like Ready, Cloning, Restoring, Deleted, or Failed. This provides better visibility into your database's current state and ongoing operations.
entry.title: Feature: New Firestore Database Statuses

entry.content:
Firestore Enterprise edition now supports Native mode and the Pipeline operations interface.

Pipeline operations are a new query interface for Firestore. This interface provides advanced query functionality that includes complex expressions. It also adds support for many new functions like min, max, substring, regex_match and array_contains_all.

With Firestore Enterprise edition in Native mode, index creation is also completely optional, streamlining the process of developing new queries.

To learn more about Pipeline operations, see the query interfaces overview.

This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Firestore Enterprise Edition now supports Native mode and the new Pipeline operations interface, offering advanced query capabilities and optional index creation for a streamlined development experience. This preview feature includes new functions like min, max, and regex_match.
entry.title: Feature: Firestore Enterprise Edition Enhancements

entry.content: Support for Object as document _id identifier.
entry.summary: We now support Object as a document _id identifier.
entry.title: Feature: Object ID Support

entry.content: The database clone feature is now supported at the General Availability (GA) level.
entry.summary: The database clone feature is now supported at the General Availability (GA) level. This feature allows for easier management of databases.
entry.title: Feature: Database Clone Feature Now Generally Available

entry.content: You can now query your databases and update data using the dedicated Gemini CLI extension for Firestore. This feature is available in beta.
entry.summary: Query and update your data using the new Gemini CLI extension for Firestore. Available in beta.
entry.title: Feature: Gemini CLI extension for Firestore

entry.content: Use Query insights to view query performance metrics for your database. This feature is now generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Query insights for Firestore are now generally available. You can use this feature to view query performance metrics for your database.
entry.title: Feature: Query insights now GA

entry.content: You can clone an existing database at a selected timestamp into a new database. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Clone your existing database to a new database.
entry.title: Feature: Clone Database
